003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client.assertion;
018
019import org.apache.juneau.rest.client.*;
020
021/**
022 * A fluent assertion class for a named HTTP response header.
023 *
024 * <p>
025 * Obtain instances via {@link ResponseAssertion#header(String)}.
026 *
027 * <p>
028 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
029 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
030 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
031 * (and possibly earlier).
032 *
033 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
034 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
035 * </ul>
036 *
037 * @since 9.2.1
038 */
039@SuppressWarnings({
040   "resource" // Eclipse resource analysis: response is borrowed; caller closes it
041})
042public final class ResponseHeaderAssertion {
043
044   private final String name;
045
046   private final RestResponse response;
047   private final ResponseAssertion parent;
048
049   ResponseHeaderAssertion(String name, RestResponse response, ResponseAssertion parent) {
050      this.name = name;
051      this.response = response;
052      this.parent = parent;
053   }
054
055   /**
056    * Asserts that this header is present in the response.
057    *
058    * @return This object (for chaining).
059    * @throws AssertionError If the header is absent.
060    */
061   public ResponseHeaderAssertion isPresent() {
062      if (response.getFirstHeader(name) == null)
063         throw new AssertionError("Expected header '" + name + "' to be present but was absent");
064      return this;
065   }
066
067   /**
068    * Asserts that this header is absent from the response.
069    *
070    * @return This object (for chaining).
071    * @throws AssertionError If the header is present.
072    */
073   public ResponseHeaderAssertion isAbsent() {
074      if (response.getFirstHeader(name) != null)
075         throw new AssertionError("Expected header '" + name + "' to be absent but was present with value: " + response.getFirstHeader(name).value());
076      return this;
077   }
078
079   /**
080    * Asserts that this header's value equals the expected value (case-sensitive).
081    *
082    * @param expected The expected value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
083    * @return This object (for chaining).
084    * @throws AssertionError If the value does not match.
085    */
086   @SuppressWarnings({
087      "java:S1201" // 'equals(String)' is an intentional fluent-assertion DSL method (asserts header equality), not an override of Object.equals(Object); renaming would break this public (beta) API.
088   })
089   public ResponseHeaderAssertion equals(String expected) {
090      var h = response.getFirstHeader(name);
091      var actual = h != null ? h.value() : null;
092      if (!expected.equals(actual))
093         throw new AssertionError("Header '" + name + "': expected '" + expected + "' but got '" + actual + "'");
094      return this;
095   }
096
097   /**
098    * Asserts that this header's value contains the given substring.
099    *
100    * @param substring The substring to look for. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
101    * @return This object (for chaining).
102    * @throws AssertionError If the value is absent or does not contain the substring.
103    */
104   public ResponseHeaderAssertion contains(String substring) {
105      var h = response.getFirstHeader(name);
106      var actual = h != null ? h.value() : null;
107      if (actual == null || !actual.contains(substring))
108         throw new AssertionError("Header '" + name + "' value '" + actual + "' does not contain '" + substring + "'");
109      return this;
110   }
111
112   /**
113    * Asserts that this header's value starts with the given prefix.
114    *
115    * @param prefix The expected prefix. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
116    * @return This object (for chaining).
117    * @throws AssertionError If the value is absent or does not start with the prefix.
118    */
119   public ResponseHeaderAssertion startsWith(String prefix) {
120      var h = response.getFirstHeader(name);
121      var actual = h != null ? h.value() : null;
122      if (actual == null || !actual.startsWith(prefix))
123         throw new AssertionError("Header '" + name + "' value '" + actual + "' does not start with '" + prefix + "'");
124      return this;
125   }
126
127   /**
128    * Asserts that this header's integer value equals the expected value.
129    *
130    * @param expected The expected integer value.
131    * @return This object (for chaining).
132    * @throws AssertionError If the value is absent, not a number, or does not match.
133    */
134   public ResponseHeaderAssertion integerEquals(int expected) {
135      var h = response.getFirstHeader(name);
136      if (h == null)
137         throw new AssertionError("Header '" + name + "' is absent");
138      int actual;
139      try {
140         actual = Integer.parseInt(h.value().trim());
141      } catch (NumberFormatException e) {
142         throw new AssertionError("Header '" + name + "' value '" + h.value() + "' is not a valid integer");
143      }
144      if (actual != expected)
145         throw new AssertionError("Header '" + name + "': expected " + expected + " but got " + actual);
146      return this;
147   }
148
149   /**
150    * Returns the parent {@link ResponseAssertion} to continue assertion chaining.
151    *
152    * @return The parent assertion. Never <jk>null</jk>.
153    */
154   public ResponseAssertion and() {
155      return parent;
156   }
157}
